coding_notes: Replicate 2 verdicts. Quantitative claims show high stability across replicates (factual-quantitative was the highest-agreement task type at 97.6% cross-vendor in r1). Within-vendor noise on the numbers is small. C2.6 (het/hom OR split): same pattern of vendors-not-naming-specific-split; OpenAI now names OR ~3.0 for hom (vs ignored r1), DeepSeek dropped the homozygote-specific number this round.
ground_truth_notes: ABCG2 ~30% urate excretion; Q141K ~50% residual; MAF EAS ~0.29-0.32, EUR ~0.09-0.11; per-allele OR ~1.7; gut dominant.

claims.claim_text: ABCG2 mediates approximately one-third (30-33%) of total uric acid excretion.

openai.value_quoted: ~30% (with explicit caveat that ABCG2-specific number is inferred)

claims.claim_text: Q141K reduces transporter activity by ~50% relative to wild-type.
Q141K minor allele frequency in East Asian populations: ~0.29-0.32.
Published OR for gout/hyperuricemia associated with Q141K is in the ~1.6-2.3 range (varying by population/method).
Homozygote (KK) gout OR is substantially higher than heterozygote (QK). Vendors that name a specific value should agree within 2x.

deepseek.value_quoted: Did not name hom-specific OR this round

gemini.value_quoted: Per-allele only, no clean het/hom split

claims.claim_text: Gut/intestine is the dominant site of ABCG2-mediated uric acid excretion (as opposed to kidney).

anthropic.value_quoted: gut/intestine dominant when ABCG2 dysfunctional; kidney handles more in absolute terms but gut is the non-redundant ABCG2 site
